    30	    make_permalink field: :number, length: 11, prefix: "H"
    31	
    32	    scope :pending, -> { with_state("pending") }
    33	    scope :ready, -> { with_state("ready") }
    34	    scope :shipped, -> { with_state("shipped") }
    35	    scope :trackable, -> { where("tracking IS NOT NULL AND tracking != ''") }
    36	    scope :with_state, ->(*state) { where(state:) }
    37	    # sort by most recent shipped_at, falling back to created_at. add "id desc" to make specs that involve this scope more deterministic.
    38	    scope :reverse_chronological, -> {
    39	      order(Arel.sql("coalesce(#{Spree::Shipment.table_name}.shipped_at, #{Spree::Shipment.table_name}.created_at) desc"), id: :desc)
    40	    }

    42	    scope :by_store, ->(store) { joins(:order).merge(Spree::Order.by_store(store)) }

    46	    self.allowed_ransackable_associations = ["order"]

    54	
    55	    def can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
    56	      order.can_ship? &&
    57	        inventory_units.all? { |iu| iu.shipped? || iu.allow_ship? || iu.canceled? } &&
    58	        (order.paid? || !Spree::Config[:require_payment_to_ship])

    61	    def can_transition_from_canceled_to_ready?

    64	
    65	    extend DisplayMoney
    66	
    67	    money_methods(
    68	      :cost, :amount, :item_cost,
    69	      :total, :total_before_tax

    71	    alias_attribute :amount, :cost
    72	
    73	    def after_cancel

    89	    # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this shipment, taking into

   101	    # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this shipment before VAT tax
   102	    # @note just like `cost`, this does not include any additional tax

   104	      total_before_tax - included_tax_total

   113	    end

   116	    # shipment and mark.

   123	    def include?(variant)

   143	    def refresh_rates
   144	      return shipping_rates if shipped?
   145	      return [] unless can_get_rates?
   146	
   147	      # StockEstimator.new assigment below will replace the current shipping_method
   148	      original_shipping_method_id = shipping_method.try!(:id)
   149	
   150	      new_rates = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new.shipping_rates(to_package)
   151	
   152	      # If one of the new rates matches the previously selected shipping
   153	      # method, select that instead of the default provided by the estimator.
   154	      # Otherwise, keep the default.
   155	      selected_rate = new_rates.detect { |rate| rate.shipping_method_id == original_shipping_method_id }
   156	      if selected_rate
   157	        new_rates.each do |rate|
   158	          rate.selected = (rate == selected_rate)
   159	        end
   160	      end
   161	
   162	      self.shipping_rates = new_rates

   168	    def select_shipping_method(shipping_method)
   169	      estimator = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new

   171	      rate = rates.detect { |detected| detected.shipping_method_id == shipping_method.id }
   172	      rate.selected = true
   173	      self.shipping_rates = [rate]
   174	    end

   188	    def selected_shipping_rate_id=(id)
   189	      return if selected_shipping_rate_id == id
   190	      new_rate = shipping_rates.detect { |rate| rate.id == id.to_i }
   191	      unless new_rate
   192	        fail(
   193	          ArgumentError,
   194	          "Could not find shipping rate id #{id} for shipment #{number}"
   195	        )
   196	      end
   197	
   198	      transaction do
   199	        selected_shipping_rate&.update!(selected: false)
   200	        new_rate.update!(selected: true)
   201	      end
   202	    end

   204	    def determine_state(order)

   207	      return "shipped" if shipped?
   208	      return "canceled" if order.canceled? || inventory_units.all?(&:canceled?)

   210	      if can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?

   217	    # Assigns the appropriate +state+ according to the following logic:

   219	    # canceled   if order is canceled
   220	    # pending    unless order is complete and +order.payment_state+ is +paid+
   221	    # shipped    if already shipped (ie. does not change the state)
   222	    # ready      all other cases
   223	    def recalculate_state
   224	      self.state =
   225	        if shipped?
   226	          "shipped"
   227	        elsif inventory_units.none?
   228	          "pending"
   229	        elsif order.canceled? || inventory_units.all?(&:canceled?)
   230	          "canceled"
   231	        elsif !order.can_ship?
   232	          "pending"
   233	        elsif can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
   234	          "ready"
   235	        else
   236	          "pending"
   237	        end
   238	    end

   240	    def set_up_inventory(state, variant, _order, line_item)

   244	        line_item_id: line_item.id

   264	    def to_package

   266	      package.shipment = self

   268	        package.add_multiple state_inventory_units, state.to_sym

   270	      package

   273	    def to_param

   279	
   280	      @tracking_url ||= shipping_method.build_tracking_url(tracking)
   281	    end

   283	    def update_amounts

   285	      persist_amounts

   289	      return unless selected_shipping_rate
   290	      self.cost = selected_shipping_rate.cost

   298	        )
   299	      end

   302	    # Update Shipment and make sure Order states follow the shipment changes
   303	    def update_attributes_and_order(params = {})
   304	      if update(params)
   305	        if params.key? :selected_shipping_rate_id
   306	          # Changing the selected Shipping Rate won't update the cost (for now)
   307	          # so we persist the Shipment#cost before running `order.recalculate`
   308	          update_amounts
   309	          order.recalculate
   310	        end
   311	
   312	        true
   313	      end
   314	    end
   315	
   316	    # Updates the state of the Shipment bypassing any callbacks.
   317	    #
   318	    # If this moves the shipment to the 'shipped' state, after_ship will be
   319	    # called.
   320	    def update_state
   321	      old_state = state
   322	      new_state = recalculate_state
   323	      if new_state != old_state
   324	        update_columns state: new_state, updated_at: Time.current
   325	        after_ship if new_state == "shipped"
   326	      end
   327	    end
   328	
   329	    def requires_shipment?
   330	      !stock_location || stock_location.fulfillable?
   331	    end
   332	
   333	    private
   334	
   335	    def finalize_pending_inventory_units
   336	      pending_units = inventory_units.select(&:pending?)
   337	      Spree::Stock::InventoryUnitsFinalizer.new(pending_units).run!
   338	    end
   339	
   340	    def after_ship
   341	      order.shipping.ship_shipment(self, suppress_mailer:)
   342	    end
   343	
   344	    def can_get_rates?
   345	      order.ship_address&.valid?
   346	    end
   347	
   348	    def manifest_restock(item)
   349	      if item.states["on_hand"].to_i > 0
   350	        stock_location.restock item.variant, item.states["on_hand"], self
   351	      end
   352	
   353	      if item.states["backordered"].to_i > 0
   354	        stock_location.restock_backordered item.variant, item.states["backordered"]
   355	      end
   356	    end
   357	
   358	    def manifest_unstock(item)
   359	      stock_location.unstock item.variant, item.quantity, self
   360	    end
   361	
   362	    def set_cost_zero_when_nil
   363	      self.cost = 0 unless cost
   364	    end
   365	
   366	    def ensure_can_destroy
   367	      if shipped? || canceled?
   368	        errors.add(:state, :cannot_destroy, state:)
   369	        throw :abort
   370	      end
   371	    end
   372	  end
   373	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  # Manage and process a payment for an order, from a specific
     5	  # source (e.g. `Spree::CreditCard`) using a specific payment method (e.g
     6	  # `Solidus::Gateway::Braintree`).
     7	  #
     8	  class Payment < Spree::Base
     9	    include Spree::Payment::Processing
    10	    include Metadata
    11	
    12	    IDENTIFIER_CHARS = (("A".."Z").to_a + ("0".."9").to_a - %w[0 1 I O]).freeze
    13	    NON_RISKY_AVS_CODES = ["B", "D", "H", "J", "M", "Q", "T", "V", "X", "Y"].freeze
    14	    RISKY_AVS_CODES = ["A", "C", "E", "F", "G", "I", "K", "L", "N", "O", "P", "R", "S", "U", "W", "Z"].freeze
    15	
    16	    belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", touch: true, inverse_of: :payments, optional: true
    17	    belongs_to :source, polymorphic: true, optional: true
    18	    belongs_to :payment_method, -> { with_discarded }, class_name: "Spree::PaymentMethod", inverse_of: :payments, optional: true
    19	
    20	    has_many :log_entries, as: :source
    21	    has_many :state_changes, as: :stateful
    22	    has_many :capture_events, class_name: "Spree::PaymentCaptureEvent"
    23	    has_many :refunds, inverse_of: :payment
    24	
    25	    before_validation :validate_source, unless: :invalid?
    26	    before_create :set_unique_identifier
    27	
    28	    after_save :create_payment_profile, if: :profiles_supported?
    29	
    30	    # update the order totals, etc.
    31	    after_save :update_order
    32	
    33	    after_create :create_eligible_credit_event
    34	
    35	    # invalidate previously entered payments
    36	    after_create :invalidate_old_payments
    37	
    38	    attr_accessor :request_env
    39	
    40	    validates :amount, numericality: true
    41	    validates :source, presence: true, if: :source_required?
    42	    validates :payment_method, presence: true
    43	
    44	    default_scope -> { order(:created_at) }
    45	
    46	    scope :from_credit_card, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::CreditCard") }
    47	    scope :with_state, ->(state) { where(state: state.to_s) }
    48	
    49	    scope :checkout, -> { with_state("checkout") }
    50	    scope :completed, -> { with_state("completed") }
    51	    scope :pending, -> { with_state("pending") }
    52	    scope :processing, -> { with_state("processing") }
    53	    scope :failed, -> { with_state("failed") }
    54	
    55	    scope :risky, -> { failed.or(where(avs_response: RISKY_AVS_CODES)).or(where.not(cvv_response_code: [nil, "", "M"])) }
    56	    scope :valid, -> { where.not(state: %w[failed invalid void]) }
    57	
    58	    scope :store_credits, -> { where(source_type: Spree::StoreCredit.to_s) }
    59	    scope :not_store_credits, -> { where(arel_table[:source_type].not_eq(Spree::StoreCredit.to_s).or(arel_table[:source_type].eq(nil))) }
    60	
    61	    include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.payment
    62	
    63	    # @return [String] this payment's response code
    64	    def transaction_id
    65	      response_code
    66	    end
    67	
    68	    # @return [String] this payment's currency
    69	    delegate :currency, to: :order
    70	
    71	    # @return [Spree::Money] this amount of this payment as money object
    72	    def money
    73	      Spree::Money.new(amount, {currency:})
    74	    end
    75	    alias_method :display_amount, :money
    76	
    77	    # Sets the amount, parsing it based on i18n settings if it is a string.
    78	    #
    79	    # @param amount [BigDecimal, String] the desired new amount
    80	    def amount=(amount)
    81	      self[:amount] =
    82	        case amount
    83	        when String
    84	          separator = I18n.t("number.currency.format.separator")
    85	          number = amount.delete("^0-9-#{separator}.").tr(separator, ".")
    86	          number.presence&.to_d
    87	        end || amount
    88	    end
    89	
    90	    # The total amount this payment can be credited.
    91	    #
    92	    # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this payment minus refunds
    93	    def credit_allowed
    94	      amount - refunds.sum(:amount)
    95	    end
    96	
    97	    # @return [Boolean] true when this payment can be credited
    98	    def can_credit?
    99	      credit_allowed > 0
   100	    end
   101	
   102	    # @return [Boolean] true when this payment has been fully refunded
   103	    def fully_refunded?
   104	      refunds.map(&:amount).sum == amount
   105	    end
   106	
   107	    # @return [Array<String>] the actions available on this payment
   108	    def actions
   109	      sa = source_actions
   110	      sa |= ["failure"] if processing?
   111	      sa
   112	    end
   113	
   114	    # @return [Object] the source of ths payment
   115	    def payment_source
   116	      res = source.is_a?(Payment) ? source.source : source
   117	      res || payment_method
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    # @return [Boolean] true when this payment is risky
   121	    def risky?
   122	      is_avs_risky? || is_cvv_risky? || state == "failed"
   123	    end
   124	
   125	    # @return [Boolean] true when this payment is risky based on address
   126	    def is_avs_risky?
   127	      return false if avs_response.blank? || NON_RISKY_AVS_CODES.include?(avs_response)
   128	      true
   129	    end
   130	
   131	    # @return [Boolean] true when this payment is risky based on cvv
   132	    def is_cvv_risky?
   133	      return false if cvv_response_code == "M"
   134	      return false if cvv_response_code.nil?
   135	      true
   136	    end
   137	
   138	    # @return [BigDecimal] the total amount captured on this payment
   139	    def captured_amount
   140	      capture_events.sum(:amount)
   141	    end
   142	
   143	    # @return [BigDecimal] the total amount left uncaptured on this payment
   144	    def uncaptured_amount
   145	      amount - captured_amount
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    # @return [Boolean] true when the payment method exists and is a store credit payment method
   149	    def store_credit?
   150	      payment_method.try!(:store_credit?)
   151	    end
   152	
   153	    private
   154	
   155	    def source_actions
   156	      return [] unless payment_source&.respond_to?(:actions)
   157	      payment_source.actions.select { |action| !payment_source.respond_to?("can_#{action}?") || payment_source.send("can_#{action}?", self) }
   158	    end
   159	
   160	    def validate_source
   161	      if source && !source.valid?
   162	        source.errors.each do |error|
   163	          field_name = I18n.t("activerecord.attributes.#{source.class.to_s.underscore}.#{error.attribute}")
   164	          errors.add(I18n.t(source.class.to_s.demodulize.underscore, scope: "spree"), "#{field_name} #{error.message}")
   165	        end
   166	      end
   167	      if errors.any?
   168	        throw :abort
   169	      end
   170	    end
   171	
   172	    def source_required?
   173	      !!payment_method&.source_required?
   174	    end
   175	
   176	    def profiles_supported?
   177	      !!payment_method.try(:payment_profiles_supported?)
   178	    end
   179	
   180	    def create_payment_profile
   181	      # Don't attempt to create on bad payments.
   182	      return if %w[invalid failed].include?(state)
   183	      # Payment profile cannot be created without source
   184	      return unless source
   185	      # Imported payments shouldn't create a payment profile.
   186	      return if source.imported
   187	
   188	      payment_method.create_profile(self)
   189	    rescue ActiveMerchant::ConnectionError => error
   190	      gateway_error error
   191	    end
   192	
   193	    def invalidate_old_payments
   194	      if !store_credit? && !["invalid", "failed"].include?(state)
   195	        order.payments.select { |payment|
   196	          payment.state == "checkout" && !payment.store_credit? && payment.id != id
   197	        }.each(&:invalidate!)
   198	      end
   199	    end
   200	
   201	    def update_order
   202	      if order.completed? || completed? || void?
   203	        order.recalculate
   204	      end
   205	    end
   206	
   207	    # Necessary because some payment gateways will refuse payments with
   208	    # duplicate IDs. We *were* using the Order number, but that's set once and
   209	    # is unchanging. What we need is a unique identifier on a per-payment basis,
   210	    # and this is it. Related to https://github.com/spree/spree/issues/1998.
   211	    # See https://github.com/spree/spree/issues/1998#issuecomment-12869105
   212	    def set_unique_identifier
   213	      loop do
   214	        self.number = generate_identifier
   215	        break unless self.class.exists?(number:)
   216	      end
   217	    end
   218	
   219	    def generate_identifier
   220	      Array.new(8) { IDENTIFIER_CHARS.sample }.join
   221	    end
   222	
   223	    def create_eligible_credit_event
   224	      # When cancelling an order, a payment with the negative amount
   225	      # of the payment total is created to refund the customer. That
   226	      # payment has a source of itself (Spree::Payment) no matter the
   227	      # type of payment getting refunded, hence the additional check
   228	      # if the source is a store credit.
   229	      if store_credit? && source.is_a?(Spree::StoreCredit)
   230	        source.update!({
   231	          action: Spree::StoreCredit::ELIGIBLE_ACTION,
   232	          action_amount: amount,
   233	          action_authorization_code: response_code
   234	        })
   235	      end
   236	    end
   237	  end
   238	end

     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  class OrderInventory
     5	    attr_accessor :order, :line_item, :variant
     6	
     7	    def initialize(order, line_item)
     8	      @order = order
     9	      @line_item = line_item
    10	      @variant = line_item.variant
    11	    end
    12	
    13	    # Only verify inventory for completed orders (as orders in frontend checkout
    14	    # have inventory assigned via +order.create_proposed_shipment+) or when
    15	    # shipment is explicitly passed
    16	    #
    17	    # In case shipment is passed the stock location should only unstock or
    18	    # restock items if the order is completed. That is so because stock items
    19	    # are always unstocked when the order is completed through +shipment.finalize+
    20	    def verify(shipment = nil)
    21	      if order.completed? || shipment.present?
    22	
    23	        existing_quantity = inventory_units.count
    24	        desired_quantity = line_item.quantity - existing_quantity
    25	        if desired_quantity > 0
    26	          shipment ||= determine_target_shipment(desired_quantity)
    27	          if shipment
    28	            add_to_shipment(shipment, desired_quantity)
    29	          else
    30	            order.create_shipments_for_line_item(line_item).each do |new_shipment|
    31	              new_shipment.finalize!
    32	            end
    33	          end
    34	        elsif desired_quantity < 0
    35	          remove(-desired_quantity, shipment)
    36	        end
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    def inventory_units
    41	      line_item.inventory_units
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    private
    45	
    46	    def remove(quantity, shipment = nil)
    47	      if shipment.present?
    48	        remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
    49	      else
    50	        remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
    51	      end
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    # Returns either one of the shipment:
    55	    #
    56	    # first unshipped that already includes this variant
    57	    # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, with availability check
    58	    # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, without availability check
    59	    def determine_target_shipment(quantity)
    60	      potential_shipments = order.shipments.order(:created_at, :id).select(&:ready_or_pending?)
    61	
    62	      potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
    63	        shipment.include?(variant)
    64	      end || begin
    65	        potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
    66	          Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, shipment.stock_location).can_supply?(quantity)
    67	        end
    68	      end || potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
    69	        variant.stock_location_ids.include?(shipment.stock_location_id)
    70	      end
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    def add_to_shipment(shipment, quantity)
    74	      pending_units = []
    75	      if variant.should_track_inventory?
    76	        on_hand, back_order = shipment.stock_location.fill_status(variant, quantity)
    77	
    78	        on_hand.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
    79	        back_order.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("backordered", variant, order, line_item) }
    80	      else
    81	        quantity.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
    82	      end
    83	
    84	      # adding to this shipment, and removing from stock_location
    85	      if order.completed?
    86	        Spree::Stock::InventoryUnitsFinalizer.new(pending_units).run!
    87	      end
    88	
    89	      quantity
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    def remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
    93	      order.shipments.each do |shipment|
    94	        break if quantity == 0
    95	        quantity -= remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
    96	      end
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    def remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
   100	      return 0 if quantity == 0 || shipment.shipped?
   101	
   102	      shipment_units = shipment.inventory_units_for_item(line_item, variant).reject do |variant_unit|
   103	        # TODO: exclude all 'shipped' states
   104	        variant_unit.state == "shipped"
   105	      end.sort_by(&:state)
   106	
   107	      removed_quantity = 0
   108	
   109	      shipment_units.each do |inventory_unit|
   110	        break if removed_quantity == quantity
   111	        inventory_unit.destroy
   112	        removed_quantity += 1
   113	      end
   114	
   115	      if shipment.inventory_units.count.zero?
   116	        order.shipments.destroy(shipment)
   117	      end
   118	
   119	      # removing this from shipment, and adding to stock_location
   120	      if order.completed?
   121	        shipment.stock_location.restock variant, removed_quantity, shipment
   122	      end
   123	
   124	      removed_quantity
   125	    end
   126	  end
   127	end

State guards are mostly split between the order state machine, `Order#completed?`, shipment readiness, and payment callbacks. I’m reading returns/cancellations, tax/adjustment, stock coordinator, and promotion condition files next because those are the likely indirect breakpoints for teardown.

     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  class Reimbursement < Spree::Base
     5	    class IncompleteReimbursementError < StandardError; end
     6	
     7	    belongs_to :order, inverse_of: :reimbursements, optional: true
     8	    belongs_to :customer_return, inverse_of: :reimbursements, touch: true, optional: true
     9	
    10	    has_many :refunds, inverse_of: :reimbursement
    11	    has_many :credits, inverse_of: :reimbursement, class_name: "Spree::Reimbursement::Credit"
    12	
    13	    has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :reimbursement
    14	
    15	    validates :order, presence: true
    16	    validate :validate_return_items_belong_to_same_order
    17	
    18	    accepts_nested_attributes_for :return_items, allow_destroy: true
    19	
    20	    before_create :generate_number
    21	    before_create :calculate_total
    22	
    23	    scope :reimbursed, -> { where(reimbursement_status: "reimbursed") }
    24	
    25	    # The reimbursement_tax_calculator property should be set to an object that responds to "call"
    26	    # and accepts a reimbursement object. Invoking "call" should update the tax fields on the
    27	    # associated ReturnItems.
    28	    # This allows a store to easily integrate with third party tax services.
    29	    class_attribute :reimbursement_tax_calculator
    30	    self.reimbursement_tax_calculator = ReimbursementTaxCalculator
    31	    # A separate attribute here allows you to use a more performant calculator for estimates
    32	    # and a different one (e.g. one that hits a 3rd party API) for the final caluclations.
    33	    class_attribute :reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator
    34	    self.reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator = ReimbursementTaxCalculator
    35	
    36	    # The reimbursement_models property should contain an array of all models that provide
    37	    # reimbursements.
    38	    # This allows a store to incorporate custom reimbursement methods that Spree doesn't know about.
    39	    # Each model must implement a "total_amount_reimbursed_for" method.
    40	    # Example:
    41	    #   Refund.total_amount_reimbursed_for(reimbursement)
    42	    # See the `reimbursement_generator` property regarding the generation of custom reimbursements.
    43	    class_attribute :reimbursement_models
    44	    self.reimbursement_models = [Spree::Refund, Spree::Reimbursement::Credit]
    45	
    46	    # The reimbursement_performer property should be set to an object that responds to the following methods:
    47	    # - #perform
    48	    # - #simulate
    49	    # see ReimbursementPerformer for details.
    50	    # This allows a store to customize their reimbursement methods and logic.
    51	    class_attribute :reimbursement_performer
    52	    self.reimbursement_performer = ReimbursementPerformer
    53	
    54	    include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.reimbursement
    55	
    56	    class << self
    57	      def build_from_customer_return(customer_return)
    58	        order = customer_return.order
    59	        order.reimbursements.build({
    60	          customer_return:,
    61	          return_items: customer_return.return_items.accepted.not_reimbursed
    62	        })
    63	      end
    64	    end
    65	
    66	    def display_total
    67	      Spree::Money.new(total, {currency: order.currency})
    68	    end
    69	
    70	    def calculated_total
    71	      # rounding down to handle edge cases for consecutive partial returns where rounding
    72	      # might cause us to try to reimburse more than was originally billed
    73	      return_items.to_a.sum(&:total).to_d.round(2, :down)
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    def paid_amount
    77	      reimbursement_models.sum do |model|
    78	        model.total_amount_reimbursed_for(self)
    79	      end
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    def unpaid_amount
    83	      total - paid_amount
    84	    end
    85	
    86	    def perform!(created_by:)
    87	      reimbursement_tax_calculator.call(self)
    88	      reload
    89	      update!(total: calculated_total)
    90	
    91	      reimbursement_performer.perform(self, created_by:)
    92	
    93	      if unpaid_amount_within_tolerance?
    94	        reimbursed!
    95	        Spree::Bus.publish :reimbursement_reimbursed, reimbursement: self
    96	      else
    97	        errored!
    98	        Spree::Bus.publish :reimbursement_errored, reimbursement: self
    99	      end
   100	
   101	      if errored?
   102	        raise IncompleteReimbursementError, I18n.t("spree.validation.unpaid_amount_not_zero", amount: unpaid_amount)
   103	      end
   104	    end
   105	
   106	    def simulate(created_by:)
   107	      reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator.call(self)
   108	      reload
   109	      update!(total: calculated_total)
   110	
   111	      reimbursement_performer.simulate(self, created_by:)
   112	    end
   113	
   114	    def return_items_requiring_exchange
   115	      return_items.select(&:exchange_required?)
   116	    end
   117	
   118	    def any_exchanges?
   119	      return_items.any?(&:exchange_processed?)
   120	    end
   121	
   122	    def all_exchanges?
   123	      return_items.all?(&:exchange_processed?)
   124	    end
   125	
   126	    # Accepts all return items, saves the reimbursement, and performs the reimbursement
   127	    #
   128	    # @api public
   129	    # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the user that is performing this action
   130	    # @return [void]
   131	    def return_all(created_by:)
   132	      return_items.each(&:accept!)
   133	      save!
   134	      perform!(created_by:)
   135	    end
   136	
   137	    # The returned category is used as the category
   138	    # for Spree::Reimbursement::Credit.default_creditable_class.
   139	    #
   140	    # @return [Spree::StoreCreditCategory]
   141	    def store_credit_category
   142	      Spree::StoreCreditCategory.find_by(name: Spree::StoreCreditCategory::REIMBURSEMENT)
   143	    end
   144	
   145	    private
   146	
   147	    def calculate_total
   148	      self.total ||= calculated_total
   149	    end
   150	
   151	    def generate_number
   152	      self.number ||= loop do
   153	        random = "RI#{Array.new(9) { rand(9) }.join}"
   154	        break random unless self.class.exists?(number: random)
   155	      end
   156	    end
   157	
   158	    def validate_return_items_belong_to_same_order
   159	      if return_items.any? { |ri| ri.inventory_unit.order_id != order_id }
   160	        errors.add(:base, :return_items_order_id_does_not_match)
   161	      end
   162	    end
   163	
   164	    # If there are multiple different reimbursement types for a single
   165	    # reimbursement we open ourselves to a one-cent rounding error for every
   166	    # type over the first one. This is due to how we round #unpaid_amount and
   167	    # how each reimbursement type will round as well. Since at this point the
   168	    # payments and credits have already been processed, we should allow the
   169	    # reimbursement to show as 'reimbursed' and not 'errored'.
   170	    def unpaid_amount_within_tolerance?

     3	module Spree
     4	  class Refund < Spree::Base
     5	    include Metadata
     6	
     7	    belongs_to :payment, inverse_of: :refunds, optional: true
     8	    belongs_to :reason, class_name: "Spree::RefundReason", foreign_key: :refund_reason_id, optional: true
     9	    belongs_to :reimbursement, inverse_of: :refunds, optional: true
    10	
    11	    has_many :log_entries, as: :source
    12	
    13	    validates :payment, presence: true
    14	    validates :reason, presence: true
    15	    validates :amount, presence: true, numericality: {greater_than: 0}
    16	
    17	    validate :amount_is_less_than_or_equal_to_allowed_amount, on: :create
    18	
    19	    attr_reader :perform_response
    20	
    21	    scope :non_reimbursement, -> { where(reimbursement_id: nil) }
    22	
    23	    delegate :currency, to: :payment
    24	
    25	    def money
    26	      Spree::Money.new(amount, {currency:})
    27	    end
    28	    alias_method :display_amount, :money
    29	
    30	    class << self
    31	      def total_amount_reimbursed_for(reimbursement)
    32	        reimbursement.refunds.to_a.sum(&:amount)
    33	      end
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    # Sets this price's amount to a new value, parsing it if the new value is
    37	    # a string.
    38	    #
    39	    # @param price [String, #to_d] a new amount
    40	    def amount=(price)
    41	      self[:amount] = Spree::LocalizedNumber.parse(price)
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    def description
    45	      payment.payment_method.name
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    # Must be called for the refund transaction to be processed.
    49	    #
    50	    # Attempts to perform the refund,
    51	    # raises an error if the refund fails.
    52	    def perform!
    53	      return true if transaction_id.present?
    54	
    55	      credit_cents = money.cents
    56	
    57	      @perform_response = process!(credit_cents)
    58	      log_entries.build(parsed_payment_response_details_with_fallback: perform_response)
    59	
    60	      self.transaction_id = perform_response.authorization
    61	      save!
    62	
    63	      update_order
    64	    end
    65	
    66	    private
    67	
    68	    # return an activemerchant response object if successful or else raise an error
    69	    def process!(credit_cents)
    70	      response = if payment.payment_method.payment_profiles_supported?
    71	        payment.payment_method.credit(credit_cents, payment.source, payment.transaction_id, {originator: self})
    72	      else
    73	        payment.payment_method.credit(credit_cents, payment.transaction_id, {originator: self})
    74	      end
    75	
    76	      if !response.success?
    77	        logger.error(I18n.t("spree.gateway_error") + "  #{response.to_yaml}")
    78	        text = response.params["message"] || response.params["response_reason_text"] || response.message
    79	        raise Core::GatewayError.new(text)
    80	      end
    81	
    82	      response
    83	    rescue ActiveMerchant::ConnectionError => error
    84	      logger.error(I18n.t("spree.gateway_error") + "  #{error.inspect}")
    85	      raise Core::GatewayError.new(I18n.t("spree.unable_to_connect_to_gateway"))
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    def amount_is_less_than_or_equal_to_allowed_amount
    89	      if payment && amount > payment.credit_allowed
    90	        errors.add(:amount, :greater_than_allowed)
    91	      end
    92	    end
    93	
    94	    def update_order
    95	      payment.order.recalculate
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	end

     3	module Spree
     4	  # Models the return of Inventory Units to a Stock Location for an Order.
     5	  #
     6	  class ReturnAuthorization < Spree::Base
     7	    include Metadata
     8	
     9	    belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", inverse_of: :return_authorizations, optional: true
    10	
    11	    has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :return_authorization, dependent: :destroy
    12	    has_many :inventory_units, through: :return_items, dependent: :nullify
    13	    has_many :customer_returns, through: :return_items
    14	
    15	    belongs_to :stock_location, optional: true
    16	    belongs_to :reason, class_name: "Spree::ReturnReason", foreign_key: :return_reason_id, optional: true
    17	
    18	    before_create :generate_number
    19	
    20	    accepts_nested_attributes_for :return_items, allow_destroy: true
    21	
    22	    validates :order, presence: true
    23	    validates :stock_location, presence: true
    24	    validate :must_have_shipped_units, on: :create
    25	    validate :no_previously_exchanged_inventory_units, on: :create
    26	
    27	    include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.return_authorization
    28	
    29	    extend DisplayMoney
    30	
    31	    money_methods :amount, :total_excluding_vat
    32	
    33	    self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["memo"]
    34	
    35	    def total_excluding_vat
    36	      return_items.sum(&:total_excluding_vat)
    37	    end
    38	
    39	    def amount
    40	      return_items.sum(:amount)
    41	    end
    42	
    43	    def currency
    44	      order.currency
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    def refundable_amount
    48	      order.item_total_before_tax + order.promo_total
    49	    end
    50	
    51	    def customer_returned_items?
    52	      customer_returns.exists?
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    def can_cancel_return_items?
    56	      return_items.any?(&:can_cancel?) || return_items.blank?
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    private
    60	
    61	    def must_have_shipped_units
    62	      if order.nil? || order.inventory_units.shipped.none?
    63	        errors.add(:order, I18n.t("spree.has_no_shipped_units"))
    64	      end
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def generate_number
    68	      self.number ||= loop do
    69	        random = "RA#{Array.new(9) { rand(9) }.join}"
    70	        break random unless self.class.exists?(number: random)
    71	      end
    72	    end
    73	
    74	    def no_previously_exchanged_inventory_units
    75	      if return_items.map(&:inventory_unit).any?(&:exchange_requested?)
    76	        errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.return_items_cannot_be_created_for_inventory_units_that_are_already_awaiting_exchange"))
    77	      end
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    def cancel_return_items
    81	      return_items.each { |item| item.cancel! if item.can_cancel? }
    82	    end
    83	  end
    84	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	# This represents an inventory unit that has been canceled from an order after it has already been completed
     4	# The reason specifies why it was canceled.
     5	# This class should encapsulate logic related to canceling inventory after order complete
     6	class Spree::UnitCancel < Spree::Base
     7	  SHORT_SHIP = "Short Ship"
     8	  DEFAULT_REASON = "Cancel"
     9	
    10	  belongs_to :inventory_unit, optional: true
    11	  has_one :adjustment, as: :source, dependent: :destroy
    12	
    13	  validates :inventory_unit, presence: true
    14	
    15	  # Creates necessary cancel adjustments for the line item.
    16	  def adjust!
    17	    raise "Adjustment is already created" if adjustment
    18	
    19	    amount = compute_amount(inventory_unit.line_item)
    20	
    21	    self.adjustment = inventory_unit.line_item.adjustments.create!(
    22	      source: self,
    23	      amount:,
    24	      order: inventory_unit.order,
    25	      label: "#{I18n.t("spree.cancellation")} - #{reason}",
    26	      finalized: true
    27	    )
    28	
    29	    inventory_unit.line_item.order.recalculate
    30	    adjustment
    31	  end
    32	
    33	  # This method is used by Adjustment#update to recalculate the cost.
    34	  def compute_amount(line_item)
    35	    raise "Adjustable does not match line item" unless line_item == inventory_unit.line_item
    36	
    37	    -weighted_line_item_amount(line_item)
    38	  end
    39	
    40	  private
    41	
    42	  def weighted_line_item_amount(line_item)
    43	    quantity_of_line_item = quantity_of_line_item(line_item)
    44	    raise ZeroDivisionError, "Line Item does not have any inventory units available to cancel" if quantity_of_line_item.zero?
    45	
    46	    line_item.total_before_tax / quantity_of_line_item
    47	  end
    48	
    49	  def quantity_of_line_item(line_item)
    50	    BigDecimal(line_item.inventory_units.not_canceled.count { |element| !element.original_return_item })
    51	  end
    52	end

     3	module Spree
     4	  module Stock
     5	    # Service class to finalize inventory units, it means unstock the desired
     6	    # quantity from related stock item and updates the given inventory units to
     7	    # not be pending.
     8	    class InventoryUnitsFinalizer
     9	      # @attr_reader [Spree::InventoryUnit] inventory_units to be finalized
    10	      attr_reader :inventory_units
    11	
    12	      # @param [Spree::InventoryUnit] inventory_units to be finalized
    13	      def initialize(inventory_units)
    14	        @inventory_units = inventory_units
    15	      end
    16	
    17	      # Finalize the inventory units, unstock and mark them as not pending.
    18	      def run!
    19	        Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
    20	          unstock_inventory_units
    21	          finalize_inventory_units
    22	        end
    23	      end
    24	
    25	      private
    26	
    27	      def finalize_inventory_units
    28	        inventory_units.map do |iu|
    29	          iu.update_columns(
    30	            pending: false,
    31	            updated_at: Time.current
    32	          )
    33	        end
    34	      end
    35	
    36	      def unstock_inventory_units
    37	        inventory_units.group_by(&:shipment).each do |shipment, units_by_shipment|
    38	          units_by_shipment.group_by(&:variant).each do |variant, units|
    39	            shipment.stock_location.unstock variant, units.count, shipment
    40	          end
    41	        end
    42	      end
    43	    end
    44	  end
    45	end

     3	module Spree
     4	  module Stock
     5	    class AvailabilityValidator < ActiveModel::Validator
     6	      def validate(line_item)
     7	        if is_valid?(line_item)
     8	          true
     9	        else
    10	          variant = line_item.variant
    11	          display_name = variant.name.to_s
    12	          display_name += %{ (#{variant.options_text})} unless variant.options_text.blank?
    13	
    14	          line_item.errors.add(:quantity, I18n.t(
    15	            "spree.selected_quantity_not_available",
    16	            item: display_name.inspect
    17	          ))
    18	          false
    19	        end
    20	      end
    21	
    22	      private
    23	
    24	      def is_valid?(line_item)
    25	        if line_item.inventory_units.empty?
    26	          Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(line_item.variant).can_supply?(line_item.quantity)
    27	        else
    28	          quantity_by_stock_location_id = line_item.inventory_units.pending.joins(:shipment).group(:stock_location_id).count
    29	          quantity_by_stock_location_id.all? do |stock_location_id, quantity|
    30	            Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(line_item.variant, stock_location_id).can_supply?(quantity)
    31	          end
    32	        end
    33	      end
    34	    end
    35	  end
    36	end

     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  module Stock
     5	    # A simple implementation of Stock Coordination
     6	    #
     7	    # The algorithm for allocating inventory is naive:
     8	    #   * For each available Stock Location
     9	    #     * Allocate as much on hand inventory as possible from this location
    10	    #     * Remove the amount allocated from the amount desired
    11	    #   * Repeat but for backordered inventory
    12	    #   * Combine allocated and on hand inventory into a single shipment per-location
    13	    #
    14	    # Allocation logic can be changed using a custom class (as
    15	    # configured in Spree::Config::stock_allocator_class )
    16	    #
    17	    # After allocation, splitters are run on each Package (as configured in
    18	    # Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters)
    19	    #
    20	    # Finally, shipping rates are calculated using the class configured as
    21	    # Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.
    22	    class SimpleCoordinator
    23	      attr_reader :order
    24	
    25	      # @api private
    26	      attr_reader :inventory_units, :splitters, :stock_locations,
    27	        :filtered_stock_locations, :inventory_units_by_variant, :desired,
    28	        :availability, :allocator, :packages
    29	
    30	      def initialize(order, inventory_units_deprecated = nil, inventory_units: nil)
    31	        if inventory_units_deprecated
    32	          Spree.deprecator.warn "Using the `inventory_units` positional " \
    33	            "argument is deprecated in favor of using the keyword argument. "
    34	
    35	          inventory_units ||= inventory_units_deprecated
    36	        end
    37	
    38	        @order = order
    39	        @inventory_units =
    40	          inventory_units || Spree::Config.stock.inventory_unit_builder_class.new(order).units
    41	        @splitters = Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters
    42	
    43	        @filtered_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_filter_class.new(load_stock_locations, order).filter
    44	        sorted_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_sorter_class.new(filtered_stock_locations).sort
    45	        @stock_locations = sorted_stock_locations
    46	
    47	        @inventory_units_by_variant = @inventory_units.group_by(&:variant)
    48	        @desired = Spree::StockQuantities.new(inventory_units_by_variant.transform_values(&:count))
    49	        @availability = Spree::Stock::Availability.new(
    50	          variants: desired.variants,
    51	          stock_locations:
    52	        )
    53	
    54	        @allocator = Spree::Config.stock.allocator_class.new(availability)
    55	      end
    56	
    57	      def shipments
    58	        @shipments ||= begin
    59	          @packages = build_packages
    60	          shipments = build_shipments
    61	
    62	          # Make sure we don't add the proposed shipments to the order
    63	          order.shipments = order.shipments - shipments
    64	
    65	          shipments
    66	        end
    67	      end
    68	
    69	      private
    70	
    71	      def load_stock_locations
    72	        Spree::StockLocation.all
    73	      end
    74	
    75	      def build_shipments
    76	        # Turn the Stock::Packages into a Shipment with rates
    77	        packages.map do |package|
    78	          shipment = package.shipment = package.to_shipment
    79	          shipment.shipping_rates = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new.shipping_rates(package)
    80	          shipment
    81	        end
    82	      end
    83	
    84	      def build_packages
    85	        # Allocate any available on hand inventory and remaining desired inventory from backorders
    86	        on_hand_packages, backordered_packages, leftover = allocator.allocate_inventory(desired)
    87	
    88	        raise Spree::Order::InsufficientStock.new(items: leftover.quantities) unless leftover.empty?
    89	
    90	        packages = stock_locations.map do |stock_location|
    91	          # Combine on_hand and backorders into a single package per-location
    92	          on_hand = on_hand_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
    93	          backordered = backordered_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
    94	
    95	          # Skip this location it has no inventory
    96	          next if on_hand.empty? && backordered.empty?
    97	
    98	          # Turn our raw quantities into a Stock::Package
    99	          package = Spree::Stock::Package.new(stock_location)
   100	          package.add_multiple(get_units(on_hand), :on_hand)
   101	          package.add_multiple(get_units(backordered), :backordered)
   102	
   103	          package
   104	        end.compact
   105	
   106	        # Split the packages
   107	        split_packages(packages)
   108	      end
   109	
   110	      def split_packages(initial_packages)
   111	        initial_packages.flat_map do |initial_package|
   112	          stock_location = initial_package.stock_location
   113	          Spree::Stock::SplitterChain.new(stock_location, @splitters).split([initial_package])
   114	        end
   115	      end
   116	
   117	      def get_units(quantities)
   118	        # Change our raw quantities back into inventory units
   119	        quantities.flat_map do |variant, quantity|
   120	          inventory_units_by_variant[variant].shift(quantity)
   121	        end
   122	      end
   123	    end
   124	  end
   125	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  module Stock
     5	    class Package
     6	      attr_reader :stock_location, :contents
     7	      attr_accessor :shipment
     8	
     9	      # @param stock_location [Spree::StockLocation] the stock location this package originates from
    10	      # @param contents [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the contents of this package
    11	      def initialize(stock_location, contents = [])
    12	        @stock_location = stock_location
    13	        @contents = contents
    14	      end
    15	
    16	      # Adds an inventory unit to this package.
    17	      #
    18	      # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] an inventory unit to be
    19	      #   added to this package
    20	      # @param state [:on_hand, :backordered] the state of the item to be
    21	      #   added to this package
    22	      def add(inventory_unit, state = :on_hand)
    23	        contents << ContentItem.new(inventory_unit, state) unless find_item(inventory_unit)
    24	      end
    25	
    26	      # Adds multiple inventory units to this package.
    27	      #
    28	      # @param inventory_units [Array<Spree::InventoryUnit>] a collection of
    29	      #   inventory units to be added to this package
    30	      # @param state [:on_hand, :backordered] the state of the items to be
    31	      #   added to this package
    32	      def add_multiple(inventory_units, state = :on_hand)
    33	        inventory_units.each { |inventory_unit| add(inventory_unit, state) }
    34	      end
    35	
    36	      # Removes a given inventory unit from this package.
    37	      #
    38	      # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] the inventory unit to be
    39	      #   removed from this package
    40	      def remove(inventory_unit)
    41	        item = find_item(inventory_unit)
    42	        @contents -= [item] if item
    43	      end
    44	
    45	      # @return [Spree::Order] the order associated with this package
    46	      def order
    47	        # Fix regression that removed package.order.
    48	        # Find it dynamically through an inventory_unit.
    49	        contents.detect { |item| !!item.try(:line_item).try(:order) }.try(:line_item).try(:order)
    50	      end
    51	
    52	      # @return [Float] the summed weight of the contents of this package
    53	      def weight
    54	        contents.sum(&:weight)
    55	      end
    56	
    57	      # @return [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the content items in this
    58	      #   package which are on hand
    59	      def on_hand
    60	        contents.select(&:on_hand?)
    61	      end
    62	
    63	      # @return [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the content items in this
    64	      #   package which are backordered
    65	      def backordered
    66	        contents.select(&:backordered?)
    67	      end
    68	
    69	      # Find a content item in this package by inventory unit and optionally
    70	      # state.
    71	      #
    72	      # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] the desired inventory
    73	      #   unit
    74	      # @param state [:backordered, :on_hand, nil] the state of the desired
    75	      #   content item, or nil for any state
    76	      def find_item(inventory_unit, state = nil)
    77	        contents.detect do |item|
    78	          item.inventory_unit == inventory_unit &&
    79	            (!state || item.state.to_s == state.to_s)
    80	        end
    81	      end
    82	
    83	      # @param state [:backordered, :on_hand, nil] the state of the content
    84	      #   items of which we want the quantity, or nil for the full quantity
    85	      # @return [Fixnum] the number of inventory units in the package,
    86	      #   counting only those in the given state if it was specified
    87	      def quantity(state = nil)
    88	        matched_contents = state.nil? ? contents : contents.select { |content| content.state.to_s == state.to_s }
    89	        matched_contents.sum(&:quantity)
    90	      end
    91	
    92	      # @return [Boolean] true if there are no inventory units in this
    93	      #   package
    94	      def empty?
    95	        quantity == 0
    96	      end
    97	
    98	      # @return [String] the currency of the order this package belongs to
    99	      def currency
   100	        order.currency
   101	      end
   102	
   103	      # @return [Array<Spree::ShippingCategory>] the shipping categories of the
   104	      #   variants in this package
   105	      def shipping_categories
   106	        Spree::ShippingCategory.where(id: shipping_category_ids)
   107	      end
   108	
   109	      # @return [ActiveRecord::Relation] the [Spree::ShippingMethod]s available
   110	      #   for this pacakge based on the stock location and shipping categories.
   111	      def shipping_methods
   112	        Spree::ShippingMethod
   113	          .with_all_shipping_category_ids(shipping_category_ids)
   114	          .available_in_stock_location(stock_location)
   115	      end
   116	
   117	      # @return [Spree::Shipment] a new shipment containing this package's
   118	      #   inventory units, with the appropriate shipping rates and associated
   119	      #   with the correct stock location
   120	      def to_shipment
   121	        # At this point we should only have one content item per inventory unit
   122	        # across the entire set of inventory units to be shipped, which has
   123	        # been taken care of by the Prioritizer
   124	        contents.each { |content_item| content_item.inventory_unit.state = content_item.state.to_s }
   125	
   126	        Spree::Shipment.new(
   127	          order:,
   128	          stock_location:,
   129	          inventory_units: contents.map(&:inventory_unit)
   130	        )

     5	    class InventoryUnitBuilder
     6	      def initialize(order)
     7	        @order = order
     8	      end

    10	      def units
    11	        @order.line_items.flat_map do |line_item|
    12	          build_units(line_item, line_item.quantity)
    13	        end
    14	      end
    15	
    16	      def missing_units_for_line_item(line_item)
    17	        quantity = line_item.quantity - line_item.inventory_units.count
    18	        build_units(line_item, quantity)
    19	      end

    21	      private

    23	      def build_units(line_item, quantity)
    24	        Array.new(quantity) do
    25	          Spree::InventoryUnit.new(
    26	            pending: true,
    27	            variant: line_item.variant,
    28	            line_item:
    29	          )
    30	        end
    31	      end
    32	    end
    33	  end

Stock and shipment teardown have several important guards: completed orders finalize stock, shipped/canceled shipments cannot be destroyed, and shipment rebuild is forbidden once complete or non-pending. I’m doing the promotion-side audit now, including both current `solidus_promotions` and `legacy_promotions`.

     3	module SolidusPromotions
     4	  # Base class for all promotion benefits.
     5	  #
     6	  # A Benefit is the active part of a promotion: once a promotion becomes
     7	  # eligible for a given promotable (order, line item, or shipment), the benefit
     8	  # determines how much discount to apply and produces the corresponding
     9	  # adjustments.
    10	  #
    11	  # Subclasses specialize the discounting target (orders, line items, or
    12	  # shipments) and usually include one of the following mixins to integrate with
    13	  # Solidus' adjustment system:
    14	  # - SolidusPromotions::Benefits::AdjustLineItem
    15	  # - SolidusPromotions::Benefits::AdjustShipment
    16	  # - SolidusPromotions::Benefits::CreateDiscountedItem
    17	  #
    18	  # A benefit can discount any object for which {#can_discount?} returns true.
    19	  # Implementors must provide a calculator via Spree::CalculatedAdjustments and
    20	  # may override methods such as {#adjustment_label}.
    21	  #
    22	  # Usage example
    23	  #
    24	  #   benefit = SolidusPromotions::Benefits::AdjustLineItem.new(promotion: promo)
    25	  #   if benefit.can_discount?(line_item)
    26	  #     discount = benefit.discount(line_item)
    27	  #     # => #<SolidusPromotions::ItemDiscount ...>
    28	  #   end
    29	  #
    30	  # @see SolidusPromotions::Promotion
    31	  # @see Spree::CalculatedAdjustments
    32	  class Benefit < Spree::Base
    33	    include Spree::Preferences::Persistable
    34	    include Spree::CalculatedAdjustments
    35	    include Spree::AdjustmentSource
    36	
    37	    before_destroy :remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders
    38	    before_destroy :raise_for_adjustments_for_completed_orders
    39	
    40	    # @!attribute [rw] promotion
    41	    #   The owning promotion.
    42	    #   @return [SolidusPromotions::Promotion]
    43	    belongs_to :promotion, inverse_of: :benefits
    44	    # @!attribute [rw] original_promotion_action
    45	    #   Back-reference to the original Solidus (Spree) promotion action, when migrated.
    46	    #   @return [Spree::PromotionAction, nil]
    47	    belongs_to :original_promotion_action, class_name: "Spree::PromotionAction", optional: true
    48	    # @!attribute [r] adjustments
    49	    #   Adjustments created by this benefit.
    50	    #   @return [ActiveRecord::Associations::CollectionProxy<Spree::Adjustment>]
    51	    has_many :adjustments, class_name: "Spree::Adjustment", as: :source, dependent: :restrict_with_error
    52	    # @!attribute [r] shipping_rate_discounts
    53	    #   Shipping-rate-level discounts generated by this benefit.
    54	    #   @return [ActiveRecord::Associations::CollectionProxy<SolidusPromotions::ShippingRateDiscount>]
    55	    has_many :shipping_rate_discounts, class_name: "SolidusPromotions::ShippingRateDiscount", inverse_of: :benefit, dependent: :restrict_with_error
    56	    # @!attribute [r] conditions
    57	    #   Conditions attached to this benefit.
    58	    #   @return [ActiveRecord::Associations::CollectionProxy<SolidusPromotions::Condition>]
    59	    has_many :conditions, class_name: "SolidusPromotions::Condition", inverse_of: :benefit, dependent: :destroy
    60	
    61	    # @!method self.of_type(type)
    62	    #   Restricts benefits to the given STI type(s).
    63	    #   @param type [String, Symbol, Class, Array<String,Symbol,Class>] a single type or list of types
    64	    #   @return [ActiveRecord::Relation<SolidusPromotions::Benefit>]
    65	    scope :of_type, ->(type) { where(type: Array.wrap(type).map(&:to_s)) }
    66	
    67	    # Base set of order-level condition classes available to all benefits.
    68	    #
    69	    # These generic order conditions apply regardless of the concrete benefit
    70	    # type, as every benefit ultimately operates within the context of an order.
    71	    # Concrete benefit subclasses may extend or override this to include
    72	    # additional applicable conditions that are specific to their discount
    73	    # target (e.g., line-item or shipment conditions).
    74	    #
    75	    # @return [Enumerable<Class<SolidusPromotions::Condition>>]
    76	    def self.applicable_conditions
    77	      SolidusPromotions::Condition.applicable_to([Spree::Order])
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    # Returns relations that should be preloaded for this condition.
    81	    #
    82	    # Override this method in subclasses to specify associations that should be eager loaded
    83	    # to avoid N+1 queries when computing discounts or performing automations.
    84	    #
    85	    # @return [Array<Symbol>] An array of association names to preload
    86	    def preload_relations
    87	      [:calculator]
    88	    end
    89	
    90	    # Whether this benefit can discount the given object.
    91	    #
    92	    # Subclasses must implement this according to the kinds
    93	    # of objects they are able to discount.
    94	    #
    95	    # @param object [Object] a potential adjustable (order, line item, or shipment)
    96	    # @return [Boolean]
    97	    # @see SolidusPromotions::Benefits::AdjustLineItem,
    98	    #      SolidusPromotions::Benefits::AdjustShipment,
    99	    #      SolidusPromotions::Benefits::CreateDiscountedItem
   100	    def can_discount?(object)
   101	      respond_to?(discount_method_for(object))
   102	    end
   103	
   104	    # Calculates and returns a discount for the given adjustable object.
   105	    #
   106	    # This method computes the discount amount using the benefit's calculator and returns
   107	    # an ItemDiscount object representing the discount to be applied. If the computed
   108	    # amount is zero, no discount is returned.
   109	    #
   110	    # @param adjustable [Object] The object to calculate the discount for (e.g., LineItem, Shipment, ShippingRate)
   111	    # @param ... [args, kwargs] Additional arguments passed to the calculator's compute method
   112	    #
   113	    # @return [Spree::Adjustment, SolidusPromotions::ShippingRateDiscount, nil] An ItemDiscount object if a discount applies, nil if the amount is zero
   114	    #
   115	    # @example Calculating a discount for a line item
   116	    #   benefit.discount(line_item)
   117	    #   # => #<Spree::Adjustment, adjustable: line_item, amount: -10.00, ...>
   118	    #
   119	    # @see #compute_amount
   120	    # @see #adjustment_label
   121	    def discount(adjustable, ...)
   122	      if can_discount?(adjustable)
   123	        send(discount_method_for(adjustable), adjustable, ...)
   124	      else
   125	        raise NotImplementedError, "Please implement #{discount_method_for(adjustable)} in your condition"
   126	      end
   127	    end
   128	
   129	    def self.inherited(klass)
   130	      def klass.method_added(method_added)
   131	        if method_added == :discount
   132	          Spree.deprecator.warn <<~MSG
   133	            Please refactor `#{name}`. You're defining `#discount`. Instead, define a method for each type of discountable
   134	            that your benefit can discount. For example:
   135	            ```
   136	            class MyBenefit < SolidusPromotions::Benefit
   137	              def can_discount?(discountable)
   138	                discountable.is_a?(Spree::LineItem)
   139	              end
   140	
   141	              def discount(order, _options = {})
   142	                amount = compute_amount(line_item, ...)
   143	                return if amount.zero?
   144	
   145	                ItemDiscount.new(
   146	                  item: line_item,
   147	                  label: adjustment_label(line_item),
   148	                  amount: amount,
   149	                  source: self
   150	                )
   151	              end
   152	            ```
   153	            can now become
   154	            ```
   155	            class MyBenefit < SolidusPromotions::Benefit
   156	              def discount_line_item(order, ...)
   157	                amount = compute_amount(line_item, ...)
   158	                return if amount.zero?
   159	
   160	                ItemDiscount.new(
   161	                  item: line_item,
   162	                  label: adjustment_label(line_item),
   163	                  amount: amount,
   164	                  source: self
   165	                )
   166	              end
   167	            end
   168	            ```
   169	          MSG
   170	        end
   171	        super
   172	      end
   173	      super
   174	    end
   175	
   176	    # Computes the discount amount for the given adjustable.
   177	    #
   178	    # Ensures the returned amount is negative and does not exceed the
   179	    # adjustable's discountable amount.
   180	    #
   181	    # @param adjustable [#discountable_amount] the adjustable to compute for
   182	    # @param ... [args, kwargs] additional arguments forwarded to the calculator
   183	    # @return [BigDecimal] a negative amount suitable for creating an adjustment
   184	    def compute_amount(adjustable, ...)
   185	      promotion_amount = calculator.compute(adjustable, ...) || Spree::ZERO
   186	      [adjustable.discountable_amount, promotion_amount.abs].min * -1
   187	    end
   188	
   189	    # Builds the localized label for adjustments created by this benefit.
   190	    #
   191	    # This method attempts to use a calculator-specific label method if available,
   192	    # falling back to a localized string key based on the adjustable's class name.
   193	    #
   194	    # ## Calculator Override
   195	    #
   196	    # Calculators can provide custom labels by implementing a method named after the
   197	    # adjustable type. For example, a calculator that discounts line items could
   198	    # implement `line_item_adjustment_label`:
   199	    #
   200	    # @example Custom calculator with adjustment label
   201	    #   class MyCalculator < Spree::Calculator
   202	    #     def compute(adjustable, *args)
   203	    #       # calculation logic
   204	    #     end
   205	    #
   206	    #     def line_item_adjustment_label(line_item, *args)
   207	    #       "Custom discount for #{line_item.product.name}"
   208	    #     end
   209	    #   end
   210	    #
   211	    # The method name follows the pattern: `{adjustable_type}_adjustment_label`
   212	    # where `{adjustable_type}` is the underscored class name of the adjustable
   213	    # (e.g., `line_item`, `shipment`, `shipping_rate`).
   214	    #
   215	    # If the calculator does not respond to the expected method, the benefit will
   216	    # fall back to using an i18n translation key based on the adjustable's class.
   217	    #
   218	    # @param adjustable [Object] the object being discounted (e.g., Spree::LineItem, Spree::Shipment)
   219	    # @param ... [args, kwargs] additional arguments forwarded to the calculator's label method
   220	    # @return [String] a localized label suitable for display in adjustments
   221	    #
   222	    # @see #adjustment_label_method_for
   223	    def adjustment_label(adjustable, ...)
   224	      if calculator.respond_to?(adjustment_label_method_for(adjustable))
   225	        calculator.send(adjustment_label_method_for(adjustable), adjustable, ...)
   226	      else
   227	        I18n.t(
   228	          "solidus_promotions.adjustment_labels.#{adjustable.class.name.demodulize.underscore}",
   229	          promotion: SolidusPromotions::Promotion.model_name.human,
   230	          promotion_customer_label: promotion.customer_label
   231	        )
   232	      end
   233	    end
   234	
   235	    # Partial path used for admin forms for this benefit type.
   236	    #
   237	    # @return [String]
   238	    def to_partial_path
   239	      "solidus_promotions/admin/benefit_fields/#{model_name.element}"
   240	    end
   241	
   242	    def level
   243	      raise NotImplementedError, "Please implement the correct interface, or include one of the `SolidusPromotions::Benefits::OrderBenefit`, " \
   244	        "`SolidusPromotions::Benefits::LineItemBenefit` or `SolidusPromotions::Benefits::ShipmentBenefit` modules"
   245	    end
   246	    deprecate :level, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
   247	
   248	    # Returns the set of condition classes that can still be attached to this benefit.
   249	    # Already-persisted conditions are excluded.
   250	    #
   251	    # @return [Set<Class<SolidusPromotions::Condition>>]
   252	    def available_conditions
   253	      self.class.applicable_conditions - conditions.select(&:persisted?)
   254	    end
   255	
   256	    # Returns the calculators allowed for this benefit type.
   257	    #
   258	    # @return [Array<Class>] calculator classes
   259	    def available_calculators
   260	      SolidusPromotions.config.promotion_calculators[self.class] || []
   261	    end
   262	
   263	    # Verifies if the promotable satisfies all applicable conditions of this benefit.
   264	    #
   265	    # When dry_run is true, an {SolidusPromotions::EligibilityResults} entry is
   266	    # recorded for each condition with success/error details; otherwise, the
   267	    # evaluation short-circuits on the first failure.
   268	    #
   269	    # @param promotable [Object] the entity being evaluated (e.g., Spree::Order, Spree::LineItem)
   270	    # @param dry_run [Boolean] whether to collect detailed eligibility information
   271	    # @return [Boolean] true when all applicable conditions are eligible
   272	    def eligible_by_applicable_conditions?(promotable, dry_run: false)
   273	      if dry_run
   274	        Spree.deprecator.warn <<~MSG
   275	          Passing `dry_run` to `#eligible_by_applicable_conditions` is deprecated. If you want to check promotion
   276	          eligibility, use the `SolidusPromotions::PromotionEligibilityChecker` service object instead.
   277	        MSG
   278	      end
   279	      conditions.map do |condition|
   280	        next unless condition.applicable?(promotable)
   281	        eligible = condition.eligible?(promotable)
   282	
   283	        break [false] if !eligible && !dry_run
   284	
   285	        if dry_run
   286	          if condition.eligibility_errors.details[:base].first
   287	            code = condition.eligibility_errors.details[:base].first[:error_code]
   288	            message = condition.eligibility_errors.full_messages.first
   289	          end
   290	          promotion.eligibility_results.add(
   291	            item: promotable,
   292	            condition: condition,
   293	            success: eligible,
   294	            code: eligible ? nil : (code || :coupon_code_unknown_error),
   295	            message: eligible ? nil : (message || I18n.t(:coupon_code_unknown_error, scope: [:solidus_promotions, :eligibility_errors]))
   296	          )
   297	        end
   298	
   299	        eligible
   300	      end.compact.all?
   301	    end
   302	
   303	    # All line items of the order that are eligible for this benefit.
   304	    #
   305	    # @param order [Spree::Order]
   306	    # @return [Array<Spree::LineItem>] eligible line items
   307	    def applicable_line_items(order)
   308	      order.discountable_line_items.select do |line_item|
   309	        eligible_by_applicable_conditions?(line_item)
   310	      end
   311	    end
   312	
   313	    # Base set of order-level condition classes available to all benefits.
   314	    #
   315	    # These generic order conditions apply regardless of the concrete benefit
   316	    # type, as every benefit ultimately operates within the context of an order.
   317	    # Concrete benefit subclasses may extend or override this to include
   318	    # additional applicable conditions that are specific to their discount
   319	    # target (e.g., line-item or shipment conditions).
   320	    #
   321	    # @return [Set<Class<SolidusPromotions::Condition>>]
   322	    def possible_conditions
   323	      Spree.deprecator.warn("Use #{self.class.name}.applicable_conditions instead.")
   324	      self.class.applicable_conditions
   325	    end
   326	
   327	    private
   328	
   329	    def discount_method_for(adjustable)
   330	      :"discount_#{adjustable.class.name.demodulize.underscore}"
   331	    end
   332	
   333	    def adjustment_label_method_for(adjustable)
   334	      :"#{adjustable.class.name.demodulize.underscore}_adjustment_label"
   335	    end
   336	
   337	    # Prevents destroying a benefit when it has adjustments on completed orders.
   338	    #
   339	    # Adds an error and aborts the destroy callback chain when such adjustments exist.
   340	    # @api private
   341	    def raise_for_adjustments_for_completed_orders
   342	      if adjustments.joins(:order).merge(Spree::Order.complete).any?
   343	        errors.add(:base, :cannot_destroy_if_order_completed)
   344	        throw(:abort)
   345	      end
   346	    end
   347	  end
   348	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Spree
     4	  # The customers cart until completed, then acts as permanent record of the transaction.
     5	  #
     6	  # `Spree::Order` is the heart of the Solidus system, as it acts as the customer's
     7	  # cart as they shop. Once an order is complete, it serves as the
     8	  # permanent record of their purchase. It has many responsibilities:
     9	  #
    10	  # * Records and validates attributes like `total` and relationships like
    11	  # `Spree::LineItem` as an ActiveRecord model.
    12	  #
    13	  # * Implements a customizable state machine to manage the lifecycle of an order.
    14	  #
    15	  # * Implements business logic to provide a single interface for quesitons like
    16	  # `checkout_allowed?` or `payment_required?`.
    17	  #
    18	  #  * Implements an interface for mutating the order with methods like
    19	  # `empty!` and `fulfill!`.
    20	  #
    21	  class Order < Spree::Base
    22	    ORDER_NUMBER_LENGTH = 9
    23	    ORDER_NUMBER_LETTERS = false
    24	    ORDER_NUMBER_PREFIX = "R"
    25	
    26	    include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.order
    27	
    28	    include Spree::Order::Payments
    29	    include Metadata
    30	
    31	    class InsufficientStock < StandardError
    32	      attr_reader :items
    33	
    34	      def initialize(message = nil, items: {})
    35	        @items = items
    36	        super(message)
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    class CannotRebuildShipments < StandardError; end
    41	
    42	    extend Spree::DisplayMoney
    43	
    44	    money_methods(
    45	      :outstanding_balance,
    46	      :item_total,
    47	      :adjustment_total,
    48	      :included_tax_total,
    49	      :additional_tax_total,
    50	      :tax_total,
    51	      :shipment_total,
    52	      :total,
    53	      :order_total_after_store_credit,
    54	      :total_available_store_credit,
    55	      :item_total_before_tax,
    56	      :shipment_total_before_tax,
    57	      :item_total_excluding_vat,
    58	      :promo_total
    59	    )
    60	    alias_method :display_ship_total, :display_shipment_total
    61	
    62	    checkout_flow do
    63	      go_to_state :address
    64	      go_to_state :delivery
    65	      go_to_state :payment, if: ->(order) { order.payment_required? }
    66	      go_to_state :confirm
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    self.allowed_ransackable_associations = %w[shipments user bill_address ship_address line_items]
    70	    self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = %w[completed_at created_at email number state payment_state shipment_state total store_id]
    71	
    72	    attr_reader :coupon_code
    73	    attr_accessor :temporary_address
    74	
    75	    attr_accessor :temporary_payment_source
    76	
    77	    # Customer info
    78	    belongs_to :user, class_name: Spree::UserClassHandle.new, optional: true
    79	
    80	    belongs_to :bill_address, foreign_key: :bill_address_id, class_name: "Spree::Address", optional: true
    81	    alias_method :billing_address, :bill_address
    82	    alias_method :billing_address=, :bill_address=
    83	
    84	    belongs_to :ship_address, foreign_key: :ship_address_id, class_name: "Spree::Address", optional: true
    85	    alias_method :shipping_address, :ship_address
    86	    alias_method :shipping_address=, :ship_address=
    87	
    88	    alias_attribute :ship_total, :shipment_total
    89	
    90	    belongs_to :store, class_name: "Spree::Store", optional: true
    91	
    92	    # Items
    93	    has_many :line_items, -> { order(:created_at, :id) }, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :order
    94	    has_many :variants, through: :line_items
    95	    has_many :products, through: :variants
    96	
    97	    # Shipping
    98	    has_many :shipments, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :order do
    99	      def states
   100	        pluck(:state).uniq
   101	      end
   102	    end
   103	    has_many :inventory_units, through: :shipments
   104	    has_many :cartons, -> { distinct }, through: :inventory_units
   105	
   106	    # Adjustments and promotions
   107	    has_many :adjustments, -> { order(:created_at) }, as: :adjustable, inverse_of: :adjustable, dependent: :destroy, autosave: true
   108	    has_many :line_item_adjustments, through: :line_items, source: :adjustments
   109	    has_many :shipment_adjustments, through: :shipments, source: :adjustments
   110	    has_many :all_adjustments,
   111	      class_name: "Spree::Adjustment",
   112	      foreign_key: :order_id,
   113	      dependent: :destroy,
   114	      inverse_of: :order
   115	
   116	    # Payments
   117	    has_many :payments, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :order
   118	    has_many :valid_store_credit_payments, -> { store_credits.valid }, inverse_of: :order, class_name: "Spree::Payment", foreign_key: :order_id
   119	
   120	    # Returns
   121	    has_many :return_authorizations, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :order
   122	    has_many :return_items, through: :inventory_units
   123	    has_many :customer_returns, -> { distinct }, through: :return_items
   124	    has_many :reimbursements, inverse_of: :order
   125	    has_many :refunds, through: :payments

   167	          super
   168	        end
   169	      end

   171	      def find_order(_lock = false)
   172	        @order = Spree::Order
   173	          .includes(line_items: [:adjustments, {variant: :images}],
   174	            payments: :payment_method,
   175	            shipments: {
   176	              shipping_rates: {shipping_method: :zones, taxes: :tax_rate}
   177	            })
   178	          .find_by!(number: params[:id])
   179	      end
   180	
   181	      def order_id
